assess-1.title: Constitutional Law — Final Quiz
assess-1.description: Test your understanding of the 1973 Constitution

questions.text: In which year was the current Constitution of Pakistan enacted?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of Pakistan was adopted on 10 April 1973.

questions.text: Which Article of the Constitution deals with Freedom of Speech?
questions.explanation: Article 19 guarantees freedom of speech and expression subject to reasonable restrictions.

questions.text: How many members are in the National Assembly of Pakistan?
questions.explanation: The National Assembly consists of 342 seats including reserved seats for women and minorities.

questions.text: Which body has the power to interpret the Constitution finally?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of Pakistan is the apex court with final judicial authority.

questions.text: Article 25A of the Constitution relates to:
questions.explanation: Article 25A mandates free and compulsory education for children aged 5-16.

assess-2.title: Family Law — Final Quiz
assess-2.description: Test your knowledge of Pakistani family laws

questions.text: Under the Muslim Family Laws Ordinance 1961, a man must obtain permission from the Arbitration Council before:
questions.explanation: Section 6 of MFLO 1961 requires Arbitration Council permission before contracting a subsequent marriage.
Khula is the right of a wife to seek dissolution of marriage, usually by returning the Mehr.

questions.text: The period of Iddat after divorce is:
questions.explanation: Iddat after talaq is generally three menstrual cycles (or 3 months for post-menopausal women).

questions.text: Who has the right of Hizanat (custody) for a son up to age 7?
questions.explanation: Under Hanafi law, the mother retains Hizanat for a son until the age of 7.

options: A gift from wife to husband
An obligatory payment by husband to wife
questions.explanation: Mehr is an obligatory gift of money or property from the husband to the wife upon marriage.

assess-3.title: Labor Law — Final Quiz
assess-3.description: Test your knowledge of Pakistani labor legislation

questions.explanation: EOBI — Employees Old-Age Benefits Institution — provides pension and other social security benefits.

questions.text: Under the Factories Act, the maximum working hours per week are:
questions.explanation: The Factories Act 1934 limits working hours to 48 hours per week for adult workers.

questions.text: The Protection Against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act was enacted in:
questions.explanation: The Harassment at Workplace Act was enacted in 2010 in Pakistan.
NIRC — National Industrial Relations Commission — adjudicates industrial disputes.

questions.text: Notice period for retrenchment under the Industrial Relations Act is:
questions.explanation: Standard notice period for retrenchment is one month or payment in lieu thereof.
